The **Senedd constituencies** ([Welsh](/wiki/Welsh_language "Welsh language"): *Etholaethau Senedd Cymru*) and, until May 2026, **Senedd electoral regions** ([Welsh](/wiki/Welsh_language "Welsh language"): *Rhanbarthau etholiadol Senedd Cymru*)[[i]](#cite_note-5) are the electoral districts used to elect [members of the Senedd](/wiki/Members_of_the_Senedd "Members of the Senedd") (MS; [Welsh](/wiki/Welsh_language "Welsh language"): *Aelodau'r Senedd or AS*) to the [Senedd](/wiki/Senedd "Senedd") (Welsh Parliament; [Welsh](/wiki/Welsh_language "Welsh language"): *Senedd Cymru*), and have been used in some form since the first election of the then *National Assembly for Wales* [in 1999](/wiki/1999_National_Assembly_for_Wales_election "1999 National Assembly for Wales election").

Since 1999, there have been forty single-member constituencies and five four-member electoral regions, electing 60 Senedd members in total.[[4]](#cite_note-2006_Order-6) From the [2026 Senedd election](/wiki/2026_Senedd_election "2026 Senedd election") in May 2026, 96 Senedd members will be elected from sixteen six-member constituencies, with the electoral regions being abolished, due to the [Senedd Reform Act](/wiki/Senedd_Cymru_(Members_and_Elections)_Act_2024 "Senedd Cymru (Members and Elections) Act 2024") of 2024. The Senedd constituencies are not used for [local government](/wiki/Local_government_in_Wales "Local government in Wales").

The constituencies were created through the [Government of Wales Act 1998](/wiki/Government_of_Wales_Act_1998 "Government of Wales Act 1998"), which established the National Assembly for Wales. The Assembly's constituencies were initially linked to the boundaries used for [UK Parliament constituencies in Wales](/wiki/UK_Parliament_constituencies_in_Wales "UK Parliament constituencies in Wales"), set by the [UK Parliament](/wiki/UK_Parliament "UK Parliament")'s [Boundary Commission for Wales](/wiki/Boundary_Commission_for_Wales "Boundary Commission for Wales"), except a delay in implementing new boundaries between the 2007 Assembly election and the subsequent [2010 UK election](/wiki/2010_United_Kingdom_general_election_in_Wales "2010 United Kingdom general election in Wales"). The [Parliamentary Voting System and Constituencies Act 2011](/wiki/Parliamentary_Voting_System_and_Constituencies_Act_2011 "Parliamentary Voting System and Constituencies Act 2011"), unlinked the two sets of constituencies, meaning any changes to one set, no longer affected the other. Subsequently, the UK Parliament [introduced new constituencies](/wiki/2023_review_of_Westminster_constituencies "2023 review of Westminster constituencies") for its [2024 UK election](/wiki/2024_United_Kingdom_general_election_in_Wales "2024 United Kingdom general election in Wales"), and the [Democracy and Boundary Commission Cymru](/wiki/Democracy_and_Boundary_Commission_Cymru "Democracy and Boundary Commission Cymru") was set up in 2024 to conduct boundary reviews of Senedd constituencies, with the constituencies not been overseen by a statutory body prior to 2024.[[5]](#cite_note-2011_Act-7) Following the [2026 boundary review](/wiki/2026_review_of_Senedd_constituencies "2026 review of Senedd constituencies"), six-member constituencies are being introduced from the [2026 Senedd election](/wiki/2026_Senedd_election "2026 Senedd election"), whilst the electoral regions are being abolished. Another review will be conducted before the 2030 election.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Senedd_constituencies_and_electoral_regions&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Establishment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Senedd_constituencies_and_electoral_regions&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Establishment")]

Following the [1997 Welsh devolution referendum](/wiki/1997_Welsh_devolution_referendum "1997 Welsh devolution referendum"), where a narrow majority voted in support of the creation of a [devolved](/wiki/Devolution_in_the_United_Kingdom "Devolution in the United Kingdom") [Welsh Assembly](/wiki/Welsh_Assembly "Welsh Assembly"), constituencies of the devolved legislature were established.

Section 2 of the [Government of Wales Act 1998](/wiki/Government_of_Wales_Act_1998 "Government of Wales Act 1998") stipulates that the constituencies for the [National Assembly for Wales](/wiki/National_Assembly_for_Wales "National Assembly for Wales") be the same as the constituencies used for elections to the [United Kingdom Parliament](/wiki/United_Kingdom_Parliament "United Kingdom Parliament").[[6]](#cite_note-8) The same act sets out the creation of five regions which would use the same borders as the five [European Parliamentary constituencies](/wiki/European_Parliamentary_Constituencies "European Parliamentary Constituencies") in Wales which themselves were set out in the European Parliamentary Constituencies (Wales) Order 1994,[[7]](#cite_note-9) used for [elections](/wiki/European_Election "European Election") to the [European Parliament](/wiki/European_Parliament "European Parliament") between [1994](/wiki/1994_European_Parliament_election_in_the_United_Kingdom "1994 European Parliament election in the United Kingdom") and [1999](/wiki/1999_European_Parliament_election_in_the_United_Kingdom "1999 European Parliament election in the United Kingdom"). The electoral regions set out are still used, despite the abolishment of the five European Parliamentary constituencies for an [all-Wales constituency](/wiki/Wales_(European_Parliament_constituency) "Wales (European Parliament constituency)"), and the withdrawal of the [UK](/wiki/UK "UK") from the [European Union](/wiki/European_Union "European Union"). Although minor border adjustments to the regions have taken place.

### 2007 change in boundaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Senedd_constituencies_and_electoral_regions&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 2007 change in boundaries")]

In 2006, the [Government of Wales Act 2006](/wiki/Government_of_Wales_Act_2006 "Government of Wales Act 2006") was enacted. When enacted the act reinforced the link between Assembly and UK Parliamentary constituencies, and that the number of electoral regions is five.

Following the [fifth periodic review of Westminster constituencies](/wiki/Fifth_periodic_review_of_Westminster_constituencies "Fifth periodic review of Westminster constituencies"), new borders for the constituencies and electoral regions were defined by the Parliamentary Constituencies and Assembly Electoral Regions (Wales) Order 2006.[[4]](#cite_note-2006_Order-6)

The order detailed the abolition of three constituencies ([Caernarfon](/wiki/Caernarfon_(National_Assembly_for_Wales_constituency) "Caernarfon (National Assembly for Wales constituency)"), [Conwy](/wiki/Conwy_(National_Assembly_for_Wales_constituency) "Conwy (National Assembly for Wales constituency)"), and [Meironnydd Nant Conwy](/wiki/Meirionnydd_Nant_Conwy_(National_Assembly_for_Wales_constituency) "Meirionnydd Nant Conwy (National Assembly for Wales constituency)")), with three new constituencies to replace them ([Aberconwy](/wiki/Aberconwy_(Senedd_constituency) "Aberconwy (Senedd constituency)"), [Arfon](/wiki/Arfon_(Senedd_constituency) "Arfon (Senedd constituency)"), and [Dwyfor Meirionnydd](/wiki/Dwyfor_Meirionnydd_(Senedd_constituency) "Dwyfor Meirionnydd (Senedd constituency)")). Nine constituencies were subject to "substantial" border adjustments involving the transfer of more than 3,000 inhabitants between constituencies. A further eight constituencies were subject to boundary changes resulting in the redistribution of fewer than 3,000 inhabitants between each constituency, and a further four constituencies were subject to minor boundary adjustments that led to minimal transfers of inhabitants between constituencies. The remaining sixteen constituencies were not subject to any boundary or name modifications.

The three new constituencies straddled the border of the electoral regions of [Mid and West Wales](/wiki/Mid_and_West_Wales_(Senedd_electoral_region) "Mid and West Wales (Senedd electoral region)") and [North Wales](/wiki/North_Wales_(Senedd_electoral_region) "North Wales (Senedd electoral region)"), leading to adjustments in the boundaries of both electoral regions, in addition to minor adjustments to the [constituency of Montgomeryshire](/wiki/Montgomeryshire_(Senedd_constituency) "Montgomeryshire (Senedd constituency)") also leading to minor regional boundary adjustments. In [south Wales](/wiki/South_Wales "South Wales"), the boundaries of the electoral regions, [South Wales West](/wiki/South_Wales_West_(Senedd_electoral_region) "South Wales West (Senedd electoral region)"), and [South Wales Central](/wiki/South_Wales_Central "South Wales Central") were altered to accommodate changes to the boundaries of [Bridgend](/wiki/Bridgend_(Senedd_constituency) "Bridgend (Senedd constituency)"), and [Vale of Glamorgan](/wiki/Vale_of_Glamorgan_(Senedd_constituency) "Vale of Glamorgan (Senedd constituency)") constituencies.

The changes in the boundaries for constituencies and electoral regions of the Senedd came into force for the [2007 National Assembly for Wales election](/wiki/2007_National_Assembly_for_Wales_election "2007 National Assembly for Wales election").

### Delinking from UK parliament constituencies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Senedd_constituencies_and_electoral_regions&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Delinking from UK parliament constituencies")]

Section 13(1) of the [Parliamentary Voting System and Constituencies Act 2011](/wiki/Parliamentary_Voting_System_and_Constituencies_Act_2011 "Parliamentary Voting System and Constituencies Act 2011") states that:[[8]](#cite_note-10)

> The Assembly constituencies are the constituencies specified in the Parliamentary
>
> Constituencies and Assembly Electoral Regions (Wales) Order 2006 (S.I. 2006/1041)24 as
> amended by—
> the Parliamentary Constituencies and Assembly Electoral Regions (Wales) (Amendment)
>
> Order 2008 (S.I. 2008/1791)

— [Parliamentary Voting System and Constituencies Act 2011](/wiki/Parliamentary_Voting_System_and_Constituencies_Act_2011 "Parliamentary Voting System and Constituencies Act 2011")

This details that any further changes to the UK Parliament constituencies in Wales specified in the act (notably the proposed reduction in constituencies to 30) will not be applied to Assembly (Senedd) constituencies.

In a session of the House of Commons where the then [secretary of state for Wales](/wiki/Secretary_of_state_for_Wales "Secretary of state for Wales"), [Cheryl Gillan](/wiki/Cheryl_Gillan "Cheryl Gillan") was questioned on the Labour party's opposition to the decoupling of the two constituencies, she replied:[[9]](#cite_note-11)

> That is a very interesting thought. Hon.
> Members are well aware that the Parliamentary Voting System and Constituencies Act 2011 broke the link between Assembly constituencies and parliamentary constituencies. I have agreed that we need to look carefully at the implications of having constituency boundaries relating to different areas and regions for UK and Assembly elections in Wales.

— [Rt. Hon Cheryl Gillan](/wiki/Cheryl_Gillan "Cheryl Gillan") [MP](/wiki/Member_of_Parliament_(UK) "Member of Parliament (UK)")

Boundaries of Senedd constituencies and electoral regions were initially not overseen by any statutory review body,[[10]](#cite_note-12) following the delinking of Senedd and UK Parliament constituencies in 2011. With the responsibility for proposing alterations to the boundaries of [UK Parliament constituencies in Wales](/wiki/UK_Parliament_constituencies_in_Wales "UK Parliament constituencies in Wales"), and reporting to the [UK Government](/wiki/UK_Government "UK Government"), lay with the [Boundary Commission for Wales](/wiki/Boundary_Commission_for_Wales "Boundary Commission for Wales"). At the time, both constituencies had the same boundaries. Organisations such as the [Electoral Reform Society Cymru](/wiki/Electoral_Reform_Society "Electoral Reform Society") indicated a preference for coterminosity (mirroring Senedd and UK Parliament constituencies, especially during the [2016 proposed reforms](/wiki/2018_periodic_review_of_Westminster_constituencies "2018 periodic review of Westminster constituencies")).[[11]](#cite_note-13) However, such coterminosity was merely desired and was not enforced by law, meaning any changes to UK Parliament constituencies in Wales did not need to be mirrored for Senedd constituencies. The [2023 review of Westminster constituencies](/wiki/2023_review_of_Westminster_constituencies "2023 review of Westminster constituencies") reduced the number of UK Parliament constituencies in Wales from 40 to 32, and were used from the [2024 UK election](/wiki/2024_United_Kingdom_general_election "2024 United Kingdom general election").[[12]](#cite_note-14) While the Senedd is to introduce larger constituencies composed of pairings of the 32 from 2026.

### 2020 renaming

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Senedd_constituencies_and_electoral_regions&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2020 renaming")]

On 6 May 2020, the [Senedd and Elections (Wales) Act 2020](/wiki/Senedd_and_Elections_(Wales)_Act_2020 "Senedd and Elections (Wales) Act 2020") came into force, renaming the Assembly constituencies and Assembly electoral regions of the [National Assembly for Wales](/wiki/National_Assembly_for_Wales "National Assembly for Wales"), to the Senedd constituencies and Senedd electoral regions of "[Senedd Cymru](/wiki/Senedd_Cymru "Senedd Cymru")" or "the [Welsh Parliament](/wiki/Welsh_Parliament "Welsh Parliament")", known in both [Welsh](/wiki/Welsh_language "Welsh language") and [English](/wiki/English_language "English language") as the [Senedd](/wiki/Senedd "Senedd").

### 2024–25 boundary review

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Senedd_constituencies_and_electoral_regions&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2024–25 boundary review")]

Main article: [2026 review of Senedd constituencies](/wiki/2026_review_of_Senedd_constituencies "2026 review of Senedd constituencies")

The [Senedd Cymru (Members and Elections) Act 2024](/wiki/Senedd_Cymru_(Members_and_Elections)_Act_2024 "Senedd Cymru (Members and Elections) Act 2024"), passed by the Senedd in May 2024,[[13]](#cite_note-15) gave the responsibility for conducting boundary reviews of Senedd constituencies to the [Democracy and Boundary Commission Cymru](/wiki/Democracy_and_Boundary_Commission_Cymru "Democracy and Boundary Commission Cymru"). The commission would be responsible to conduct reviews for specifically both the [2026 Senedd election](/wiki/2026_Senedd_election "2026 Senedd election") and the 2030 Senedd election, as well as regular reviews thereafter.[[14]](#cite_note-16) The [2026 review](/wiki/2026_review_of_Senedd_constituencies "2026 review of Senedd constituencies") was time-constricted,[[15]](#cite_note-Price-2025-17) therefore the 2030 review, the first full boundary review in 18 years, would provide the commission with more flexibility.[[16]](#cite_note-BBC_News-2025-18)[[17]](#cite_note-19)

The Senedd Cymru (Members and Elections) Act 2024 legislated that the [2026 election](/wiki/Next_Senedd_election "Next Senedd election") should use 16 six-member constituencies, replacing the existing 40 constituencies and five regions, with the Democracy and Boundary Commission Cymru conducting the review.[[18]](#cite_note-20) The law set out that they must be contiguous pairings of the [32 UK Parliament constituencies used in Wales](/wiki/List_of_UK_Parliament_constituencies_in_Wales "List of UK Parliament constituencies in Wales") since [2024](/wiki/2024_United_Kingdom_general_election_in_Wales "2024 United Kingdom general election in Wales"),[[19]](#cite_note-initial-proposals-2024-21) and to only use a single name in both Welsh and English, unless it could be considered unacceptable for a single name.[[20]](#cite_note-new-constituencies-announced-22) The review commenced in July 2024, with [initial proposals](/wiki/2026_review_of_Senedd_constituencies#Initial_proposals_(September_2024) "2026 review of Senedd constituencies") revealed in September 2024,[[19]](#cite_note-initial-proposals-2024-21)[[20]](#cite_note-new-constituencies-announced-22) and [revised proposals](/wiki/2026_review_of_Senedd_constituencies#Revised_proposals_(December_2024) "2026 review of Senedd constituencies") in December 2024. A major change in the revised proposals was the use of mostly Welsh-language names only to meet the condition in law, therefore all (except four) had only one name, its Welsh name.[[21]](#cite_note-BBC_News-2024-23) The decision received support from [Cymdeithas yr Iaith](/wiki/Cymdeithas_yr_Iaith "Cymdeithas yr Iaith"), but opposition from [Andrew RT Davies](/wiki/Andrew_RT_Davies "Andrew RT Davies"), former leader of the [Welsh Conservatives](/wiki/Welsh_Conservatives "Welsh Conservatives").[[21]](#cite_note-BBC_News-2024-23)[[22]](#cite_note-ITV_News-2024-24) In March 2025, the commission published its [final determinations](/wiki/2026_review_of_Senedd_constituencies#Final_proposals_(March_2025) "2026 review of Senedd constituencies"),[[23]](#cite_note-25) for the 16 constituencies (see [§ Proposed constituencies (2026)](#Proposed_constituencies_(2026))). The original initial proposal boundaries were re-adopted, while all constituencies were made to use [Welsh-only names](/wiki/2026_review_of_Senedd_constituencies#Welsh-only_names "2026 review of Senedd constituencies"), with a reiteration of both support and opposition from Cymdeithas and Davies respectively, as well as consultation respondents.[[16]](#cite_note-BBC_News-2025-18) By law, the Senedd must put these final determinations into effect for the 2026 Senedd election without alteration.[[15]](#cite_note-Price-2025-17)

## Additional Member System (1999–2026)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Senedd_constituencies_and_electoral_regions&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Additional Member System (1999–2026)")]

Under the Additional Member System in place until May 2026, there are forty single-member constituencies and five four-member regions. The five electoral regions are: [Mid and West Wales](/wiki/Mid_and_West_Wales_(Senedd_electoral_region) "Mid and West Wales (Senedd electoral region)"), [North Wales](/wiki/North_Wales_(Senedd_electoral_region) "North Wales (Senedd electoral region)"), [South Wales Central](/wiki/South_Wales_Central "South Wales Central"), [South Wales East](/wiki/South_Wales_East "South Wales East"), and [South Wales West](/wiki/South_Wales_West_(Senedd_electoral_region) "South Wales West (Senedd electoral region)"); each region roughly contains 500,000 people and each constituency contains a population of around 60,000. The forty constituencies are listed below.[[4]](#cite_note-2006_Order-6) The final election under the Additional Member System was the [2025 Caerphilly by-election](/wiki/2025_Caerphilly_by-election "2025 Caerphilly by-election") and the final general election was the [2021 Senedd election](/wiki/2021_Senedd_election "2021 Senedd election"), with boundaries that were also used for the Assembly elections of [2007](/wiki/2007_National_Assembly_for_Wales_election "2007 National Assembly for Wales election"), [2011](/wiki/2011_National_Assembly_for_Wales_election "2011 National Assembly for Wales election"), and [2016](/wiki/2016_National_Assembly_for_Wales_election "2016 National Assembly for Wales election").

Senedd constituencies are grouped into electoral regions consisting of between seven and nine constituencies. An [additional member system](/wiki/Additional_member_system "Additional member system") is used to elect four additional Members of the Senedd from each region, in addition to the MSs elected by the constituencies. The Electoral Regions boundaries were based upon the pre-1999 [European Parliament constituencies](/wiki/European_Parliament_constituencies_in_the_United_Kingdom "European Parliament constituencies in the United Kingdom"). At each [general election](/wiki/General_election "General election") of the Senedd, each elector had two votes, one constituency vote and one regional party-list vote. Each constituency elected one Member by the [first past the post](/wiki/First_past_the_post "First past the post") (single-member district plurality, SMDP) system, and the additional Senedd seats were filled from regional [closed party lists](/wiki/Closed_party_list "Closed party list"), under the [D'Hondt method](/wiki/D%27Hondt_method "D'Hondt method"), with constituency results being taken into account, to produce a degree of [proportional representation](/wiki/Proportional_representation "Proportional representation") for each region. Altogether, the sixty Members of the Senedd were elected from the forty constituencies and five electoral regions, creating a Senedd of forty constituency MSs and twenty additional MSs, with every [constituent](/wiki/Electoral_district "Electoral district") represented by one constituency member and four regional members.

The constituencies were the same as those used for [elections to the House of Commons of the United Kingdom](/wiki/House_of_Commons_of_the_United_Kingdom "House of Commons of the United Kingdom") until the [2024 United Kingdom general election](/wiki/2024_United_Kingdom_general_election "2024 United Kingdom general election") but not linked to them. The borders of each constituency were drawn using Local government boundaries, defined in Parliamentary Voting System and Constituencies Act 2011 as "the boundaries of counties, county boroughs, electoral divisions, communities and community wards".[[5]](#cite_note-2011_Act-7)[[24]](#cite_note-27)

Former constituencies and regions (1999–2007)

Between the first election in [1999](/wiki/1999_National_Assembly_for_Wales_election "1999 National Assembly for Wales election") for the then [National Assembly for Wales](/wiki/National_Assembly_for_Wales "National Assembly for Wales"), to the [2007 election](/wiki/2007_National_Assembly_for_Wales_election "2007 National Assembly for Wales election"), there were three former constituencies. These constituencies were replaced at the 2007 election, with new boundaries and names.

Three constituency names, [Conwy](/wiki/Conwy_(Wales_Assembly_constituency) "Conwy (Wales Assembly constituency)"), [Caernarfon](/wiki/Caernarfon_(Wales_Assembly_constituency) "Caernarfon (Wales Assembly constituency)"), and [Meirionydd Nant Conwy](/wiki/Meirionnydd_Nant_Conwy_(National_Assembly_for_Wales_constituency) "Meirionnydd Nant Conwy (National Assembly for Wales constituency)"), became historic, and the new boundaries defined three constituencies with new names: [Arfon](/wiki/Arfon_(Senedd_constituency) "Arfon (Senedd constituency)"), [Dwyfor Meirionnydd](/wiki/Dwyfor_Meirionnydd_(Senedd_constituency) "Dwyfor Meirionnydd (Senedd constituency)"), and [Aberconwy](/wiki/Aberconwy_(Senedd_constituency) "Aberconwy (Senedd constituency)"). Generally, the new boundaries define each constituency taking into account local government ward boundaries, and define constituencies close to equal in terms of the sizes of their electorates.

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Senedd_constituencies_and_electoral_regions&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-1)** Consisting of between 7-9 constituencies.
2. **[^](#cite_ref-26)** Relevance to the new Welsh name of the Senedd constituency

1. **[^](#cite_ref-5)** They are known as **Senedd constituencies** and **Senedd electoral regions** in the [Senedd and Elections (Wales) Act 2020](/wiki/Senedd_and_Elections_(Wales)_Act_2020 "Senedd and Elections (Wales) Act 2020") and revised [Government of Wales Act 2006](/wiki/Government_of_Wales_Act_2006 "Government of Wales Act 2006"),[[1]](#cite_note-2)[[2]](#cite_note-3) but may be alternatively titled by Senedd Research,[[3]](#cite_note-2021_Data-4) and English-language news sources as **Welsh Parliament constituencies and electoral regions**, or **Senedd Cymru constituencies and electoral regions**. The districts were formerly known as **National Assembly for Wales constituencies and electoral regions**, or informally, (Welsh) **Assembly constituencies and electoral regions** from its establishment in 1999 until May 2020, when it was renamed to its current name(s).
